reason: Both answers compute identical, correct unit economics (LTV $3,200, 4:1 LTV:CAC, ~8.3mo payback) and correctly identify 3% monthly churn (~31% annual) as the key weakness that investors will scrutinize, matching all the key points. The substantive content is essentially the same. The only difference is framing: A opens with a more confident "Yes, fundable" bottom line consistent with the actual (strong) unit economics, while B hedges with "Yes, marginally," which slightly undersells numbers that clear benchmarks comfortably (4:1 vs 3:1 target, 8.3mo vs 12mo threshold). This is a minor calibration difference; both are otherwise comparable in structure, clarity, and actionable takeaway (fix/trend churn or show NRR before scaling spend).
